description: Predict the atmospheric re-entry time and location of a spent rocket body (CZ-5B R/B, NORAD 59001) using the orbital elements and physical properties provided in ~/Desktop/ssa_bulletin_59001.txt. Simulate the orbital decay in GMAT using an atmospheric drag model (JacchiaRoberts or MSISE) with F10.7 = 150, propagating the orbit until it reaches a re-entry threshold of 120 km altitude. Save the simulation script to ~/GMAT_output/reentry_sim.script and generate a prediction report at ~/GMAT_output/reentry_prediction_report.txt containing the following fields: reentry_utc, elapsed_days, reentry_latitude_deg, reentry_longitude_deg, reentry_altitude_km, and final_period_min.
